front: Play: a note left on a pillow says 'We're all out of Corn Flakes. F.U.' — signed by the neat-freak roommate
Play: a poker game breaks up after one of the players disinfects the playing cards
Play: one roommate throws a plate of linguini (not spaghetti) at the wall
Play: the title characters try double-dating with the British Pigeon sisters
Play: one character takes green pills; his roommate jokes 'Maybe they were vitamins. He could be the healthiest one in the room!'
Play: Private Arnold Epstein forces the abusive Sergeant Toomey to do 200 push-ups in front of the platoon
work: Eugene Jerome Trilogy (Brighton Beach Memoirs / Biloxi Blues / Broadway Bound)

front: Play: a bride complains her husband won't walk in the title fashion in Washington Square Park; he protests it was 17 degrees
Play: a leaking skylight and the intrusions of the eccentric neighbor Victor Velasco disturb a pair of newlyweds
Play: the fish-restaurant owner Barney Cashman tries and fails to seduce three women in his mother's apartment
back: The Last of the Red-Hot Lovers (Neil Simon)
work: The Last of the Red-Hot Lovers

front: Play: Deputy Mayor Charlie shoots himself in the earlobe while on Valium; the song 'La Bamba' is required in all productions
Play: elderly vaudeville team Lewis and Clark reunite to do a 'Doctor's office' sketch but keep poking and spitting on each other
Play: fifteen-and-a-half-year-old Jay and his brother Arty stay with their mentally challenged Aunt Bella and cruel grandmother
Author: wrote a musical based on Fellini's Nights of Cabiria; Bob Fosse choreographed 'Big Spender' for the film version
